channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Prime Video announces May 1 release date for ‘Sapne Vs Everyone’ Season 2 on global platform
item.description: Amazon Prime Video has officially announced the release date for the second season of its popular Hindi‑language web series ‘Sapne Vs Everyone’, which will premiere worldwide on May 1. The show, creat...

item.title: Tanzania Strengthens 10% Loan Program Through Digital System
item.description: The Tanzanian government has seen good progress with the WEZESHA digital platform, which allows people to apply for loans, track repayments, and monitor activities. Dr Festo Dugange, the Deputy Minist...

item.title: FIFA president confirms Iran's participation in 2026 World Cup despite regional conflict
item.description: FIFA president Gianni Infantino has assured that Iran will definitely participate in the 2026 FIFA World Cup, which is co-hosted by Canada, the United States, and Mexico. Speaking at an economic confe...

item.title: Philippine and US Troops Demonstrate HIMARS Rocket System in SALAKNIB 2026 Live Fire Exercise
item.description: The Philippine Army Artillery Regiment, together with the 25th Infantry Division of the United States Army Pacific (USARPAC), successfully conducted a live fire exercise using the M142 High Mobility A...

item.title: UDA defends Cabinet Secretaries amid opposition calls for dismissal
item.description: On Thursday, April 16, 2026, UDA spokesperson Hassan Omar publicly defended Cabinet Secretaries Wandayi and Kinyanjui after opposition parties called for their dismissal. The move comes amid ongoing d...

item.title: Ababu Namwamba Enjoys Family Moments in Uganda as He Begins His Role as High Commissioner
item.description: Former Cabinet Secretary and now Kenya’s High Commissioner to Uganda, Ababu Namwamba, has captured public attention after his partner, Amy Lucia, shared sweet family photos on social media. The pictur...

item.title: NASA Confirms Asteroid Apophis Will Have a Close Approach to Earth
item.description: NASA has confirmed that the asteroid Apophis, nicknamed the 'God of Chaos,' is set to make a close approach to Earth in the coming years. Although it is unlikely to collide with our planet, its proxim...

item.title: Chinese National Zhang Kequn Sentenced to One Year in Prison for Smuggling Over 2,000 Live Queen Ants from Kenya
item.description: A Chinese national called Zhang Kequn has been sentenced to one year in jail by a Kenyan court after he tried to smuggle more than 2,000 live queen garden ants out of the country. The man was arrested...

item.title: Biovac expands Cape Town facilities to boost African vaccine production
item.description: Biovac, the South African vaccine manufacturer, has unveiled an ambitious expansion plan at its Cape Town facility with backing from the European Investment Bank (EIB) Group and the International Fina...

item.title: Federal Court Orders CDA to Draft Regulations for Islamabad’s Informal Settlements
item.description: On April 16, 2026, the Federal Constitutional Court (FCC) in Islamabad instructed the Capital Development Authority (CDA) to finalise rules for regularising informal settlements in the city. This dire...

item.title: Federal Jury Rules Live Nation and Ticketmaster Maintained Illegal Monopoly in Ticketing
item.description: A Manhattan federal jury has concluded that Live Nation and its subsidiary Ticketmaster illegally maintained monopoly power over the ticketing market. This verdict stems from an antitrust lawsuit file...

item.title: Hopoate criticised for unusual play during NRL match
item.description: During a recent NRL match, Lehi Hopoate drew attention for a controversial move when he passed the ball backwards to teammate Brandon Wakeham while over the try-line. The incident, occurring in the mi...

item.title: Scottish Retailers Criticise SNP Proposal to Cap Essential Food Prices
item.description: The Scottish Retail Consortium (SRC) has strongly criticised the Scottish National Party's (SNP) proposal to impose price caps on essential food items, calling it a '1970s-style gimmick'. SRC deputy h...

item.title: Rethinking Tanzania’s healthcare: Why one hospital cannot serve all
item.description: Muhimbili National Hospital is the pride of Tanzanian healthcare, established way back in 1905 and grown to be the top medical hub in the country. But now, the government plans to spend Sh1.2 trillion...

item.title: Democrat Chris Pappas leads rivals in fundraising for New Hampshire Senate race
item.description: Democratic Representative Chris Pappas has built a significant financial lead in the 2026 New Hampshire Senate race as he seeks to replace retiring Senator Jeanne Shaheen. Pappas’s campaign raised abo...

item.title: COA affirms disallowance of OVP’s ₱73M confidential funds for lack of proof of proper use
item.description: The Commission on Audit (COA) en banc unanimously denied the appeal of the Office of the Vice President (OVP) regarding the disallowance of ₱73 million worth of confidential funds used in 2022. In its...

item.title: Two women, including BJP leader, robbed of gold chains in Salem
item.description: On April 16, 2026, in Salem, unidentified criminals snatched gold chains from two women, one of whom is S. Jayalakshmi, aged 41, the president of the BJP Omalur East union. Jayalakshmi was returning h...

item.title: Hassan Omar Defends Energy and Trade CSs Amid Fuel Scandal as Opposition Calls for Sacking
item.description: In a strong show of party solidarity, UDA Secretary General Hassan Omar has come out to defend Cabinet Secretaries Opiyo Wandayi of Energy and Lee Kinyanjui of Trade and Industry. This follows intense...

item.title: Stadium Access Concerns Raised Ahead of 2027 AFCON in Tanzania
item.description: Mhe. Asha Baraka, a Special Seats MP in Tanzania, has highlighted serious concerns regarding infrastructure readiness for the 2027 Africa Cup of Nations (AFCON). During a parliamentary debate on the P...

item.title: Absa Bank Tanzania increases unsecured loan limit to support salaried employees
item.description: Absa Bank Tanzania imeshuka hatua kubwa ya kupanua mikopo isiyo na dhamana kwa wafanyakazi wa sekta binafsi. Sasa wateja wanaokidhi vigezo wanaweza kupata mkopo hadi Sh200 million, kutoka Sh150 millio...
